title: Use of comprehensive geriatric assessment in older cancer patients: recommendations from the SIOG task force on CGA
authors_year: Extermann, Aapro, Bernabei, Cohen, Droz, Lichtman, Mor, Monfardini, Repetto, Sorbye, Topinkova, 2005
design: Systematic evidence review and consensus recommendations (adapted Oxford levels of evidence) from an eight-member SIOG task force addressing four key questions about CGA in older cancer patients.
population: Older cancer patients (soft age limit ~70); evidence base drawn largely from community-dwelling elderly and geriatric RCTs, with limited cancer-specific data.
key_eligibility: Recommends screening for geriatric problems in cancer patients beyond age 70 (a 'soft limit')
Systematic review of Medline literature through February 2003 rated by adapted Oxford criteria
outcomes: Concludes there is strong evidence that CGA detects problems missed by routine assessment and that CGA improves function and reduces hospitalization in the elderly; heterogeneous evidence for survival and cost-effectiveness; corroborative but limited cancer-specific evidence. Recommends that functional assessment be more extensive than ECOG performance status (adding ADL/IADL and a performance test such as timed get-up-and-go), plus screening for depression and cognition, and that CGA include follow-up. Cannot recommend any single tool above others.
grounds_rubric_claims: Comprehensive geriatric assessment should be used in older cancer patients to detect problems missed by standard oncologic assessment
Functional assessment should go beyond ECOG performance status (ADL/IADL, performance measures)
Geriatric screening is recommended beyond age ~70 as a soft limit

verbatim_quotes.quote: A CGA, with or without screening, and with follow-up, should be used in older cancer patients, in order to detect unaddressed problems, improve their functional status, and possibly their survival.
verbatim_quotes.supports: Core SIOG recommendation to use CGA in older cancer patients

verbatim_quotes.quote: functional status assessment should be more extensive than the ECOG performance status evaluation and may include ADL/IADL, and/or a performance test such as the timed get up and go.
verbatim_quotes.supports: CGA/GA should extend beyond ECOG performance status

verbatim_quotes.quote: Based on retrospective evidence that the incidence of geriatric problems increases sharply after 70 in cancer patients, we recommend screening for them beyond that age. This should be considered a soft limit.
verbatim_quotes.supports: Age ~70 soft threshold for geriatric screening

caveats: Explicitly labeled 'recommendations' not guidelines; evidence for specific CGA components is weak and largely extrapolated from non-cancer geriatric trials. No single tool endorsed. Publications only through early 2003.
